mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents using standardized communication protocols. It simplifies deployment complexity, enhances security, and reduces operational overhead, making it ideal for production environments. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes and potential resource overhead.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface, which is necessary for managing the deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

```

> This command downloads the CLI, makes it executable, and moves it to the local bin.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using Google Cloud with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --num-nodes=3 --zone=us-central1-a --cluster-version=1.30

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --zone=us-central1-a

```

> This command fetches the cluster credentials and merges them into your kubeconfig.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Install Kagenti on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step deploys Kagenti to your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti --create-namespace

```

> Add the Kagenti Helm repository.
> This command installs Kagenti in a dedicated namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for the Kagenti deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/autoscale.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This applies the autoscaling configuration.
> This applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti deployment from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and data.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> This command uninstalls the Kagenti release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations and data. This step will upgrade Kagenti to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kagenti -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.status.phase == "Running") | .metadata.name' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti

kubectl get pods -n kagenti

```

> Drain all running pods before upgrade.
> This command upgrades Kagenti to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n kagenti -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.status.phase == "Running") | .metadata.name' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to connect to the server: dial tcp <IP>:<PORT>: i/o timeout'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes cluster is not reachable, possibly due to network issues.

**Fix:**
Check your network settings and ensure that the cluster is running. Use 'gcloud container clusters list' to verify.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install the release: ...'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Helm chart version is incorrect or the repository is not added.

**Fix:**
Ensure you have added the correct Helm repository and are using the correct version.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the application is failing to start, possibly due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources allocated for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or size in your Kubernetes cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for autoscaling and enhanced monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n kagenti' showing all pods in 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
